fact: April 1 is exactly 91 days into the year - that's exactly 13 weeks of perfect calendar math!
In the Northern Hemisphere, tree sap is typically flowing like crazy right now, making maple syrup season peak around this time!
Robins and other birds are everywhere because April 1 falls right in the middle of peak bird migration season when millions of birds are zooming north!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this date - nature's perfect compass!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this is when leaves start changing colors and falling, making it their autumn kickoff time!
Earthworms go absolutely bonkers around April 1 in northern climates, wiggling up to the surface as the soil finally warms up after winter!
This date falls in the middle of Aries season in the zodiac calendar!
Cherry blossom trees in Japan typically hit their peak bloom right around now, creating pink snow when the petals fall!
April 1 sits exactly 10 days after the spring equinox, when day and night were perfectly balanced - now days are winning the battle!
In northern regions, this is often prime time for 'sugar snow' - the perfect sticky snow that's amazing for building snowmen because it's wet and packable!
Frogs and toads usually start their loudest croaking concerts of the year around this date as they wake up from winter hibernation!
